title: permuteDE
text: permuteDE designed run Unix-based operating systems macOS linux. permuteDE installation currently requires remotes BiocManager installation GitHub Bioconductor packages. Run following commands install various dependencies used permuteDE: First, install remotes (installing GitHub packages) isn’t already installed: , install BiocManager (installing bioconductor packages) isn’t already installed: , install permuteDE:
code: if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes") if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager") remotes::install_github("corceslab/permuteDE", ref="main", repos = BiocManager::repositories(), upgrade = "never")

title: permuteDE
text: tutorial provides basic example run permuteDE, R package intended help users assess differential expression comparisons can trusted met skepticism. permuteDE applicable sequencing data suitable differential expression analysis. takes input Seurat object, SingleCellExperiment object, matrix (see Advanced Options details input object types). Detailed parameter definitions available Functions tab. Differential expression analyses susceptible false positives, can difficult prioritize robust results study. permuteDE based premise differential expression results likely validated subsequent experiments come comparisons higher number significant differentially expressed features expected chance. use permutation testing allows permuteDE generate distribution estimating many false positive significant differentially expressed features occur chance alone. permuteDE proceeds two main steps. First, user use function runDE() run differential expression analysis using one several available methods. Second, results passed permuteDE() function, performs permutation test. ’ll need following packages installed run tutorial:
code: library(Seurat) library(permuteDE)

title: permuteDE
text: permuteDE takes input Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, matrix. matrix provided, may either cell-level matrix pre-generated pseudobulk matrix. tutorial uses Seurat object; details related object types, see Advanced Options. permuteDE expects raw counts, normalization performed differential expression analysis depending selected method. demonstrate run permuteDE, ’ll use small sample dataset consisting mouse brain snRNA-seq data, stored Seurat object.
code: # Load dataset from permuteDE package data("sample_data")  # Seurat object containing a count matrix, UMAP coordinates, and cell metadata plotDimReduction(reduction = sample_data@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings,                  split_labels = sample_data$cell_type,                  color_by = "split")

title: permuteDE
text: permuteDE proceeds two main steps, corresponding function: (1) runDE() (2) permuteDE().

title: permuteDE
text: Function runDE() run differential expression analysis compare two groups. default, runDE() generate pseudobulk matrices using metadata provided parameters replicate_labels (representing biological replicate) , optionally, split_labels (representing division data, often cell types). pseudobulk matrices returned part output runDE(), can also extracted separately using function getPseudobulk(). Alternately, users may opt run cell-level tests instead pseudobulk tests (set parameter pseudobulk “none”) provide pre-generated pseudobulk matrices (set parameter pseudobulk “supplied”). See Advanced Options details. Function runDE() allows user run several different methods differential expression analysis, adjusting parameters de_method de_test: edgeR: “LRT”, “QLF”, “exact” DESeq2: “LRT”, “Wald” limma: “trend”, “voom”, “wilcox_cpm”, “wilcox_cpm_log” presto: “wilcox_cpm”, “wilcox_cpm_log” BPCells: “wilcox_cpm”, “wilcox_cpm_log” default, runDE() run differential expression analysis using edgeR LRT. precise execution differential expression analysis can adjusted using parameter de_params pass parameters selected method. function parallelized multiple splits assessed, efficiency improves n_cores increased, number splits. output list containing following elements: DE_results Dataframe containing DE results feature, split PB_values using pseudobulk data, list feature x replicate matri(ces) containing pseudobulk values feature, one matrix per split cell_values Alternately, using cell-level data, list feature x cell matri(ces) containing counts feature, one matrix per split metadata List recording characteristics data runtime parameters List recording parameter values used
code: runDE_output <- runDE(object = sample_data,                       replicate_labels = "sample",                       group_labels = "group",                       split_labels = "cell_type",                       reference_group = "WT",                       n_cores = 2) #> 2026-06-24 23:24:07 : Generating 6 pseudobulk matrices.. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:07 : Fetching feature x cell matrix using 'RNA' assay and 'counts' layer.. #> Excluded between 19.43% (340 features) and 69.6% (1218 features) of features in each split. #> Highest % of features excluded in split 'Microglia'. #> Excluded between 1.89% (6632 reads) and 21.72% (49437 reads) of reads in each split. #> Highest % of reads excluded in split 'Microglia'. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:08 : Running DE on 6 matrices.. head(runDE_output$DE_results) #>   feature        lfc       pvalue       padj      split #> 1   Stmn1  2.1530904 7.127649e-05 0.04077015 Astrocytes #> 2    Pan3 -1.5612588 2.635217e-04 0.07536720 Astrocytes #> 3  Slc4a4 -0.6058453 9.404665e-04 0.17931562 Astrocytes #> 4   Pcsk2  1.7141881 1.798391e-03 0.25716996 Astrocytes #> 5    Rpl3  1.4697542 2.957641e-03 0.32330591 Astrocytes #> 6    Fat3 -0.8415779 3.565231e-03 0.32330591 Astrocytes

title: permuteDE
text: Function permuteDE() take output function runDE(), permute group labels rerun differential expression analysis way across number iterations set parameter n_iterations. parameters inherited runDE() function call. iteration, number significant differentially expressed features counted, forming distribution “true” number significantly differentially expressed features compared using permutation test. permutation test yields p-value suggests probability observing number significant features greater equal one output runDE() function two groups truly different. default number iterations (set parameter n_iterations) 1000. However, smaller datasets one number possible combinations group labels less 1000, permutation test run using possible combinations repeats. function highly parallelized, efficiency greatly improves n_cores increased. output list containing following elements: permutation_test_results Dataframe containing permutation test results split permutation_DE_summary Dataframe containing permutation DE summary metrics split permutation_DE_results parameter return_all TRUE, dataframe DE results feature, split, iteration (note substantially increases size output) metadata List recording characteristics data runtime parameters List recording parameter values used
code: permuteDE_output <- permuteDE(input = runDE_output,                               n_cores = 2) #> 2026-06-24 23:24:09 : Differential expression results for group WT vs. Tg across 6 pseudobulk matrices.. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:09 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split Astrocytes (1/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:09 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split Astrocytes (1/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:09 : Running 70 permutations for split Astrocytes (1/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:12 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split Excitatory Neurons (2/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:12 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split Excitatory Neurons (2/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:12 : Running 70 permutations for split Excitatory Neurons (2/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:18 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split Inhibitory Neurons (3/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:18 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split Inhibitory Neurons (3/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:18 : Running 70 permutations for split Inhibitory Neurons (3/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:24 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split Microglia (4/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:24 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split Microglia (4/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:24 : Running 70 permutations for split Microglia (4/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:27 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split Oligodendrocytes (5/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:27 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split Oligodendrocytes (5/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:27 : Running 70 permutations for split Oligodendrocytes (5/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:30 : Input 1000 for parameter 'n_combinations' exceeds the number of possible combinations 70. Only 70 combinations will be generated for split OPCs (6/6). #> 2026-06-24 23:24:30 : Generating 70 of 70 possible combinations for split OPCs (6/6).. #> 2026-06-24 23:24:30 : Running 70 permutations for split OPCs (6/6).. head(permuteDE_output$permutation_test_results) #>                split runDE_n_sig     pvalue n_iterations #> 1         Astrocytes           1 0.28571429           70 #> 2 Excitatory Neurons           8 0.08571429           70 #> 3 Inhibitory Neurons           7 0.02857143           70 #> 4          Microglia           1 0.25714286           70 #> 5   Oligodendrocytes           5 0.11428571           70 #> 6               OPCs           7 0.11428571           70 head(permuteDE_output$permutation_DE_summary) #>        split permutation reference_group_overlap non_reference_group_overlap #> 1 Astrocytes           1                    0.50                        0.50 #> 2 Astrocytes           2                    0.25                        0.25 #> 3 Astrocytes           3                    0.50                        0.50 #> 4 Astrocytes           4                    0.75                        0.75 #> 5 Astrocytes           5                    0.50                        0.50 #> 6 Astrocytes           6                    0.25                        0.25 #>   n_sig min_lfc_sig max_lfc_sig min_lfc_all max_lfc_all #> 1     1  -0.7063576  -0.7063576   -1.306694    2.303966 #> 2     0          NA          NA   -1.918125    1.173536 #> 3     0          NA          NA   -1.128684    1.283791 #> 4     4  -1.5885933   2.7290607   -1.588593    2.729061 #> 5     0          NA          NA   -2.501939    1.356584 #> 6     0          NA          NA   -1.374724    1.068373

title: permuteDE
text: permuteDE several built-plotting functions help display results functions runDE() permuteDE(). plots modifiable ggplot outputs. generate volcano plots split assessed runDE() use function plotVolcano(). generate volcano plot specific split subset splits, set parameter use_splits relevant split name(s).  generate histograms permutation test (single split), use function plotHistogram(). histograms display distribution number significant differentially expressed features iteration permutation test, dashed line “true” results fall distribution.generate histogram specific split subset splits, set parameter use_splits relevant split name(s).  One way summarise results permuteDE color UMAP number differentially expressed features split (case, cell type).  plot, cell types grayed permutation test p-value > 0.05.  can color p-values permutation test.  function can also used analogously Seurat::FeaturePlot() show values single feature across cells.  plot results single feature, use function plotFeature(). default, function apply CPM normalization (using edgeR::cpm()), can changed using parameter normalization_method.
code: # Generate a list of volcano plots volcano_plots <- plotVolcano(input = runDE_output) # Display examples volcano_plots[["Inhibitory Neurons"]] | volcano_plots[["Oligodendrocytes"]] # Generate a list of histograms histogram_plots <- plotHistogram(input = permuteDE_output) # Display examples histogram_plots[["Inhibitory Neurons"]] | histogram_plots[["Oligodendrocytes"]] # Example UMAP showing number of DE features for each split plotDimReduction(reduction = sample_data@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings,                  input = permuteDE_output,                  split_labels = sample_data$cell_type,                  color_by = "n_sig") # Example UMAP showing number of DE features for each split that passes permutation testing threshold plotDimReduction(reduction = sample_data@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings,                  input = permuteDE_output,                  split_labels = sample_data$cell_type,                  color_by = "n_sig",                  permutation_test_alpha = 0.05) # Example UMAP showing permutation testing p-value for each split plotDimReduction(reduction = sample_data@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings,                  input = permuteDE_output,                  split_labels = sample_data$cell_type,                  color_by = "pvalue",                  palette = "frozen") # Example UMAP showing permutation testing p-value for each split plotDimReduction(reduction = sample_data@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings,                  feature_values = sample_data@assays$RNA$counts["App",],                  feature_name = "*App*",                  color_by = "feature") # Generate a list of plots feature_plots <- plotFeature(input = runDE_output,                              feature = "App",                              use_splits = c("Inhibitory Neurons", "Oligodendrocytes")) # Display examples feature_plots[["Inhibitory Neurons"]] | feature_plots[["Oligodendrocytes"]]

title: permuteDE
text: Function runDE() accepts three input object types: Seurat objects, SingleCellExperiment objects, matrices.

title: permuteDE
text: Seurat objects, “RNA” assay used input provided parameter use_assay. input provided parameter use_slot, “counts” default slot (Seurat v4) layer (Seurat v5) used. Seurat v5 objects multiple layers (e.g., “counts.1”, “counts.2”, “counts.2”..) different subsets cells stored assay. Currently, permuteDE requires single layer containing cells assay. cases, please re-organize Seurat object prior running permuteDE. Seurat objects, input parameters replicate_labels, group_labels, split_labels may either string indicating name metadata column containing labels character vector containing labels order cells.

title: permuteDE
text: SingleCellExperiment objects, use_assay parameter needed. provided, set “counts”. Please ensure relevant assay matrix includes row column names. SingleCellExperiment objects, input parameters replicate_labels, group_labels, split_labels may either string indicating name metadata column containing labels character vector containing labels order cells.

title: permuteDE
text: matrix inputs, provided matrix may either cell-level matrix (feature x cell) pre-generated pseudobulk matrix (feature x replicate). permuteDE functions expect raw counts. pre-generated pseudobulk matrix provided, parameter pseudobulk must set “supplied”. matrix inputs, required input parameters replicate_labels, group_labels, split_labels must provided either: data.frame parameter metadata column names designated replicate_labels, group_labels, split_labels Directly replicate_labels, group_labels, split_labels vectors containing labels order, corresponding column matrix ’s example apply function runDE() feature x cell matrix: pre-generated pseudobulk matrix:
code: # Extract count matrix from the sample dataset counts_matrix <- sample_data@assays$RNA$counts # Extract relevant metadata as a vector replicates <- sample_data$sample groups <- sample_data$group splits <- sample_data$cell_type  # Run  runDE_output <- runDE(object = counts_matrix,                       replicate_labels = replicates,                       group_labels = groups,                       split_labels = splits,                       reference_group = "WT",                       n_cores = 2) # Pre-generate pseudobulk matrix pb_matrix_list <- getPseudobulk(object = sample_data,                                 replicate_labels = "sample",                                 split_labels = "cell_type",                                 n_cores = 2) pb_matrix <- pb_matrix_list$PB_values[["Oligodendrocytes"]]  # Extract relevant metadata as a vector replicates <- colnames(pb_matrix) groups <- sample_data$group[match(replicates, paste0("rep_", sample_data$sample))]  # Run  runDE_output <- runDE(object = pb_matrix,                       replicate_labels = replicates,                       group_labels = groups,                       reference_group = "WT",                       pseudobulk = "supplied",                       n_cores = 2)

title: permuteDE
text: run differential expression analysis accounting multiple factors/covariates, set parameter design function runDE() character string providing desired model formula. Important notes: Complex model formulas compatible Wilcoxon rank sum test default, results group comparison returned; see results covariates set parameter de_params list(return_all_coefficients = TRUE) parameter return_raw_de TRUE example comparing groups within sample data additionally correcting batch effects. cases, additional parameter adjustments needed run permutation test specified model formula.
code: runDE_output <- runDE(object = sample_data,                       replicate_labels = "sample",                       group_labels = "group",                       split_labels = "cell_type",                       reference_group = "WT",                       design = "~ batch + group",                       n_cores = 2) permuteDE_output <- permuteDE(input = runDE_output,                               n_cores = 2)

what: Comparisons within the same replicates (e.g., cell type comparisons)
title: permuteDE
text: default usage runDE() permit duplicate replicate labels across different comparison groups. Therefore, compare cell populations wherein set replicates shared across two groups interest (e.g., two different cell types states set samples), set parameter replicate_labels function runDE() concatenation replicate labels group labels. , can run either unpaired paired test:
code: # Add concatenated metadata column sample_data$sample_cell_type <- paste(sample_data$sample, sample_data$cell_type, sep = "_")

previous_headings: Advanced Options > Complex designs > Comparisons within the same replicates (e.g., cell type comparisons)
title: permuteDE
text: example comparing gene expression excitatory vs. inhibitory neurons sample dataset:
code: runDE_output <- runDE(object = sample_data,                       replicate_labels = "sample_cell_type",                       group_labels = "cell_type",                       use_cells = colnames(sample_data)[sample_data$cell_type %in% c("Excitatory Neurons", "Inhibitory Neurons")],                       reference_group = "Excitatory Neurons",                       n_cores = 2)  permuteDE_output <- permuteDE(input = runDE_output,                               n_cores = 2)

previous_headings: Advanced Options > Complex designs > Comparisons within the same replicates (e.g., cell type comparisons)
title: permuteDE
text: run paired comparison, set parameter design function runDE() additive model formula without interaction term. example, compare gene expression excitatory vs. inhibitory neurons sample dataset, model formula ~ sample + cell_type. running function permuteDE() paired comparisons, important provide correct input parameter permute_within, group labels shuffled separately within paired set. example, permute_within set "sample".
code: runDE_output <- runDE(object = sample_data,                       replicate_labels = "sample_cell_type",                       group_labels = "cell_type",                       use_cells = colnames(sample_data)[sample_data$cell_type %in% c("Excitatory Neurons", "Inhibitory Neurons")],                       reference_group = "Excitatory Neurons",                       design = "~ sample + cell_type",                       n_cores = 2) permuteDE_output <- permuteDE(input = runDE_output,                               permute_within = "sample",                               n_cores = 2)

title: permuteDE
text: default significance level used permuteDE alpha = 0.05 multiple comparison correction using FDR = 0.05. method multiple comparison correction can changed using parameter p_adjust_method (permitted values, see stats::p.adjust.methods). default, permuteDE also requires significant differentially expressed features |LFC| > 0.5 (parameter lfc_threshold). Parameter p_adjust_method can also accept value "fdrtool", advanced users wish apply fdrtool p-value adjustment. default, fdrtool applied raw p-values. DE method DESeq2 using "Wald" test, users can apply fdrtool z-scores setting parameter de_params list(fdrtool = list(statistic = "zscore")).

title: permuteDE
text: permuteDE uses various filters reduce number DE comparisons: Parameter min_cells_per_split indicates minimum number cells within one split. Defaults 100. Parameter min_cells_per_replicate indicates minimum number cells within one replicate split. Defaults 10. Parameter min_replicates_per_split indicates minimum number distinct replicates represented within one split. Defaults 6. Parameter min_replicates_per_group indicates minimum number distinct replicates represented within two comparison groups. Defaults 3. Parameter min_cells_per_feature indicates minimum number cells (within split) expression feature. Defaults 10. Parameter min_prop_cells_per_feature indicates minimum proportion cells (within split) expression feature. Defaults 0.1.

title: permuteDE
text: highly encourage users use pseudobulk differential expression analysis, reduces incidence false positives. However, permuteDE also compatible cell-level tests. run function runDE() without pseudobulking, treating cells statistical sample size, set parameter pseudobulk “none”. works input object type, including cell x feature matrices. Please note size returned output substantially larger, since cell x feature matrices returned output can subsequently passed function permuteDE(). running cell-level tests, still recommend users provide biological replicate labels function permuteDE() using parameter permute_by, group labels permuted, cells biological replicate separated. words, permutation occurs biological replicate level, even cell-level tests. Input permute_by must either column name already present output dataframe $metadata$group_key function runDE(), vector values corresponding row dataframe. input provided parameter permute_by, group labels permuted across cells regardless biological replicate. cell-level tests input count matrix stored BPCells IterableMatrix (can greatly improve computational efficiency), differential method currently compatible BPCells, uses function BPCells::marker_features(). cases, please specify parameters de_method = "BPCells" de_test = "wilcox_log_cpm" de_test = "wilcox_cpm".
code: runDE_output <- runDE(object = sample_data,                       group_labels = "group",                       split_labels = "cell_type",                       reference_group = "WT",                       pseudobulk = "none",                       n_cores = 2) # Grab sample labels for each row of group key sample_labels <- sample_data@meta.data[runDE_output$metadata$group_key$replicate, "sample"] # Provide to parameter 'permute_by' permuteDE_output <- permuteDE(input = runDE_output,                               permute_by = sample_labels,                               n_cores = 2)

what: Computational efficiency considerations for atlas-scale datasets
title: permuteDE
text: multi-million cell datasets, consider splitting dataset multiple objects (e.g., major cell type) running permuteDE functions getPseudobulk() runDE(), rather relying parameter split_labels. Moreover, consider storing input count matrices BPCells IterableMatrix format (within Seurat object).

title: permuteDE
text: Four color palettes included package. can called using function permuteDEpalette(), setting parameter swatch TRUE display palette.
code: permuteDEpalette(type = "discrete",                    n = 10,                    palette = "choir",                    swatch = TRUE)

text: permuteDE designed run Unix-based operating systems macOS linux. permuteDE installation currently requires remotes BiocManager installation GitHub Bioconductor packages. Run following commands install various dependencies used permuteDE: First, install remotes (installing GitHub packages) isn’t already installed: , install BiocManager (installing bioconductor packages) isn’t already installed: , install permuteDE: Notes: Installation complete 2 minutes. package supported macOS Linux. Package dependencies can found “DESCRIPTION” file.
code: if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes") if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager") remotes::install_github("corceslab/permuteDE", ref="main", repos = BiocManager::repositories(), upgrade = "never")

title: permuteDE: Permutation testing for differential expression analysis (www.permuteDE.com)
text: Please see tutorial. takes less 10 minutes run standard laptop.

title: permuteDE: Permutation testing for differential expression analysis (www.permuteDE.com)
text: permuteDE takes input Seurat object, SingleCellExperiment object, matrix. permuteDE enables user run several different methods differential expression analysis using runDE() function. encourage users run pseudobulk differential expression methods decrease number likely false positives, permuteDE also compatible cell-level tests. results passed permuteDE() function. Across number iterations, permuteDE shuffles group labels given set replicates performs pseudobulk differential expression permuted groups. metric used permutation test number significantly differentially expressed features true group comparison vs. comparisons use permuted group labels. See tutorial details.  permuteDE developed maintained Corces Lab Gladstone Institutes.

what: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
title: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
text: Given two groups replicates, function calculates number unique ways divide replicates two groups preserving group sizes. formula : C = n!/(k!(n-k)!). (see base function choose)

title: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
code: countCombinations(   object = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   replicate_labels = NULL,   group_labels = NULL,   use_cells = NULL,   n_replicates = NULL,   n_group1 = NULL )

title: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
text: object optional 'Seurat' 'SingleCellExperiment' object. NULL, countCombinations expect either (1) vector input parameters replicate_label group_label (2) numeric input parameters n_replicates n_group1. metadata optional dataframe containing relevant metadata columns corresponding data provided parameter object. Default = NULL looks metadata object provided inputs. replicate_labels string indicating name metadata column containing biological replicate labels character vector containing biological replicate labels order. group_labels string indicating name column containing two comparison group labels character vector containing comparison labels order. use_cells vector cell names subset prior calculating possible group comibinations. Default = NULL use cells. n_replicates numeric value indicating total number replicates. Defaults NULL. n_group1 numeric value indicating number replicates one group (matter ). Defaults NULL.

title: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
text: Returns numeric value indicating number possible group combinations.

title: Calculate the number of possible group combinations — countCombinations
text: Users may provide input three ways: (1) vector replicate labels vector group labels (order) (2) total number replicates (n) number replicates one group (k) (matter group). (3) Column names indicating replicate group metadata columns provided 'Seurat' 'SingleCellExperiment' object provided metadata dataframe.

title: Generate combinations — getCombinations
text: set replicates belong two groups, function generate set randomized group combinations.

title: Generate combinations — getCombinations
code: getCombinations(   object = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   replicate_labels = NULL,   group_labels = NULL,   use_cells = NULL,   n_replicates = NULL,   n_group1 = NULL,   n_combinations = 1000,   confound_check = NULL,   progress_message = "",   random_seed = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate combinations — getCombinations
text: object optional 'Seurat' 'SingleCellExperiment' object. NULL, countCombinations expect either (1) vector input parameters replicate_label group_label (2) numeric input parameters n_replicates n_group1. metadata optional dataframe containing relevant metadata columns corresponding data provided parameter object. Default = NULL looks metadata object provided inputs. replicate_labels string indicating name metadata column containing biological replicate labels character vector containing biological replicate labels order. group_labels string indicating name column containing two comparison group labels character vector containing comparison labels order. use_cells vector cell names subset prior calculating possible group comibinations. Default = NULL use cells. n_replicates numeric value indicating total number replicates. Alternately, vector can provided generate combinations shuffling separately within multiple sets. Defaults NULL. n_group1 numeric value indicating number replicates one group (matter ). Alternately, vector can provided generate combinations shuffling separately within multiple sets. Defaults NULL. n_combinations numeric value indicating number combinations generate. Defaults 1000. confound_check optional dataframe covariates exclude permutations perfectly confounded. Defaults NULL. progress_message character string indicating additional progress messaging (internal use). Defaults "". random_seed numeric value indicating random seed used. Defaults 1. verbose Boolean value indicating whether use verbose output execution function. Defaults TRUE. Can set FALSE cleaner output.

title: Generate combinations — getCombinations
text: Returns matrix column contains combination index values indicating replicates assign first group.

title: Generate combinations — getCombinations
text: Users may provide input three ways: (1) vector replicate labels vector group labels (order) (2) total number replicates (n) number replicates one group (k) (matter group). (3) Column names indicating replicate group metadata columns provided 'Seurat' 'SingleCellExperiment' object provided metadata dataframe.

what: Generate histogram plots of permutation test results — getHistograms
title: Generate histogram plots of permutation test results — getHistograms
text: function takes output function permuteDE creates list containing histogram plot showing permutation test result split. histogram includes vertical line observed number DE features unpermuted "true" comparison.

title: Generate histogram plots of permutation test results — getHistograms
code: getHistograms(input, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, label_pvalue = TRUE)

title: Generate histogram plots of permutation test results — getHistograms
text: input Output function permuteDE. title Character string indicating plot title. Default = NULL sets title automatically split. subtitle Character string indicating plot subtitle. Default = NULL automatically generates subtitle. label_pvalue Boolean value indicating whether label permutation test p-value. Defaults TRUE.

title: Generate histogram plots of permutation test results — getHistograms
text: named list ggplot2 objects, element corresponds split.

title: Calculate pseudobulk values — getPseudobulk
text: function generate pseudobulk expression matri(ces) provided Seurat object, SingleCellExperiment object, feature x cell matrix. Multiple pseudobulk matrices can generated across different divisions dataset, cell types.

title: Calculate pseudobulk values — getPseudobulk
code: getPseudobulk(   object,   metadata = NULL,   replicate_labels = NULL,   split_labels = NULL,   use_cells = NULL,   min_cells_per_split = 100,   min_cells_per_replicate = 10,   min_replicates_per_split = 6,   min_cells_per_feature = 10,   min_prop_cells_per_feature = 0.1,   filter = TRUE,   pseudobulk = "generate",   use_assay = NULL,   use_layer = NULL,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate pseudobulk values — getPseudobulk
text: object object class Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, matrix. Data supplied class matrix feature x cell matrix. metadata optional dataframe containing relevant metadata columns corresponding data provided parameter object. Default = NULL looks metadata object provided inputs. replicate_labels string indicating name metadata column containing biological replicate labels character vector containing biological replicate labels order. biological replicate labels used construct/define pseudobulks. Default = NULL allowed pseudobulk "none". split_labels string indicating name column split cells prior pseudobulking performing differential expression (e.g., cell types). Alternately, character vector containing split labels cell order. Results returned unique value indicated split_labels. Default = NULL generate single pseudobulk matrix includes cells. use_cells vector cell names subset object prior subsequent pseudobulk steps. Default = NULL use cells. min_cells_per_split numeric value indicating minimum number cells within one split. Pseudobulk steps performed splits fewer cells. Defaults 100. min_cells_per_replicate numeric value indicating minimum number cells within one replicate one split. Pseudobulk steps performed replicates fewer cells split. Defaults 10. min_replicates_per_split numeric value indicating minimum number distinct replicates represented within one split. Pseudobulk steps performed splits fewer replicates. Defaults 6. min_cells_per_feature numeric value indicating minimum number cells (within split) expression feature. Pseudobulk expression calculated features expressed fewer cells. Defaults 10. min_prop_cells_per_feature numeric value indicating minimum proportion cells (within split) expression feature. Pseudobulk expression calculated features expressed fewer cells. Defaults 0.1. filter Boolean value indicating whether remove features matrices output (TRUE) simply list features meet criteria part metadata (FALSE). Defaults TRUE. pseudobulk string indicating whether actually pseudobulk ("generate"), simply apply filtering thresholds ("none"). Defaults "generate". use_assay string indicating assay use provided object. Default = NULL choose current active assay Seurat objects counts assay SingleCellExperiment objects. use_layer Seurat objects, string vector indicating layer (previously known slot) use provided object. Default = NULL use counts layer. n_cores numeric value indicating number cores use parallelization. Default = NULL use number available cores minus 2. verbose Boolean value indicating whether use verbose output execution function. Defaults TRUE. Can set FALSE cleaner output.

title: Calculate pseudobulk values — getPseudobulk
text: Returns list containing following elements: PB_values list feature x replicate matri(ces) containing pseudobulk values feature, one matrix per split metadata Dataframe record quality control metrics split parameters List recording parameter values used

what: Generate volcano plots from differential expression results — getVolcanos
title: Generate volcano plots from differential expression results — getVolcanos
text: function takes output function runDE creates list containing volcano plot subset differential expression (DE) results. DE results subset "split" column, split produces volcano plot.

title: Generate volcano plots from differential expression results — getVolcanos
code: getVolcanos(   input,   alpha = 0.05,   lfc_threshold = 0.5,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   n_max_label = 10,   center = TRUE )

title: Generate volcano plots from differential expression results — getVolcanos
text: input Output function runDE list containing (minimum) dataframe named "DE_results" including columns: gene Gene identifiers (character). lfc Log2 fold change values (numeric). padj Adjusted p-values (numeric). split (Optionally) Grouping variable used subset results (character factor). alpha numeric value indicating significance level used permutation test comparisons number differentially expressed features. Defaults 0.05. lfc_threshold numeric value indicating minimum absolute value log fold change gene counted "hit". Defaults 0.5. Set 0 disregard log fold change counting hits. title Character string indicating plot title. Default = NULL sets title automatically split. subtitle Character string indicating plot subtitle. Default = NULL automatically generates subtitle describing DE method used. n_max_label numeric value indicating many top significant DE features label. Defaults 10. center Boolean value indicating whether center x-axis 0. Defaults TRUE.

title: Generate volcano plots from differential expression results — getVolcanos
text: named list ggplot2 objects, element corresponds volcano plot one split DE results.

title: Generate color palette — palette_permuteDE
text: type character string indicating palette type. Permitted values "discrete" "gradient". Defaults discrete. n Number colors. Default = NULL return pre-set colors palette. palette_name character string indicating palette name. Permitted values "choir", "archr", "corces_cold", "corces_warm". Default = NULL use "choir" type "discrete" "corces_cold" type "gradient. swatch Boolean value indicating whether plot swatch palette.

title: Generate color palette — palette_permuteDE
text: Returns vector n hex values.

title: Generate color palette — palette_permuteDE
text: "choir" palette adapted function  CHOIR::CHOIRpalette R package CHOIR (Sant et al. 2025). "archr" palette adapted palette "stallion" R package ArchR (Granja & Corces et al. 2020).

title: Perform permutation test — permuteDE
text: function performs permutation test shuffling group labels given set replicates performing pseudobulk differential expression permuted groups. metric used permutation test number significantly differentially expressed features given significance level alpha log fold change threshold lfc_threshold.

title: Perform permutation test — permuteDE
code: permuteDE(   input,   alpha = 0.05,   lfc_threshold = 0,   n_iterations = 1000,   use_splits = NULL,   permute_by = NULL,   permute_within = NULL,   min_DE = 1,   return_all = FALSE,   random_seed = 1,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Perform permutation test — permuteDE
text: input Output function runDE: list containing differential expression results, pseudobulk values, parameters used. alpha numeric value indicating significance level used permutation test comparisons number differentially expressed features. Defaults 0.05. lfc_threshold numeric value indicating minimum absolute value log fold change feature counted "hit". Default = 0 disregards log fold change counting hits. n_iterations numeric value indicating number iterations run permutation test. Defaults 1000. Computational time increases approximately linearly number iterations. use_splits vector containing names splits use. Defaults NULL, try splits. permute_by optional character string indicating name column input$metadata$group_key indicating partitions data within group labels shuffled together unit. Alternately, vector ordered according values input$metadata$group_key. specific use cases cell-level tests. Default = NULL shuffle group labels across replicates. permute_within optional character string indicating name column input$metadata$group_key indicating partitions data within group labels shuffled separately. Alternately, vector ordered according values input$metadata$group_key. specific use cases cell-level tests. Default = NULL shuffle group labels across replicates. min_DE numeric value indicating minimum number differentially expressed features true group labels split, permutations run. Defaults 1. Set 0 run permutation test splits, regardless true number DEGs. return_all Boolean value indicating whether store return DE results (log fold changes p-values per feature per split) every single permutation. Defaults = FALSE return high-level permutation test results. Note setting TRUE substantially increase size returned output. random_seed numeric value indicating random seed used. Defaults 1. n_cores numeric value indicating number cores use parallelization. Default = NULL use number available cores minus 2. verbose Boolean value indicating whether use verbose output execution function. Defaults TRUE. Can set FALSE cleaner output.

title: Perform permutation test — permuteDE
text: Returns list containing following elements: permutation_test_results Dataframe containing permutation test results split permutation_DE_summary Dataframe containing permutation DE summary metrics split permutation_DE_results parameter 'return_all' TRUE, dataframe DE results feature, split, iteration metadata List recording additional characteristics data: number significant DE features runDE, group indices iteration permutation test, runtime parameters List recording parameter values used

title: Perform permutation test — permuteDE
text: input, function requires output function runDE containing pseudobulk (cell-level) values feature differential expression results true group labels. Permutation always performed replicate level, rather cell level, performed without replacement, iteration unique permutation group labels. Notably, permutation test pass judgment individual feature, rather, intended assess many false positive significant differentially expressed features can expected chance. addition, can used characterize log fold change significance observed false positives help users prioritize reliable DE results.

Alternately, provide vector color values use starting values color palette. swatch Boolean value indicating whether plot swatch palette.

text: function generate dimensionality reduction plot colored according selected metric.

title: Plot dimensionality reduction — plotDimReduction
code: plotDimReduction(   reduction,   input = NULL,   split_labels = NULL,   feature_values = NULL,   feature_name = NULL,   use_cells = NULL,   color_by = NULL,   permutation_test_alpha = 1,   label_splits = FALSE,   label_statistics = FALSE,   palette = NULL,   color_limits = NULL,   fix_coords = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot dimensionality reduction — plotDimReduction
text: reduction Dimensionality reduction cell coordinates. two columns, first two used. input Output function permuteDE. split_labels character vector containing split labels cell order. Default = NULL assume cells belong split. feature_values vector containing value feature cell order, used color_by = "feature". feature_name string indicating name plotted feature color_by = "feature". Used legend title. use_cells vector cell names subset dimensionality reduction cell coordinates . Default = NULL use cells. color_by character vector indicating metric use color split. Permitted values "n_sig" (number significant DE features), "pvalue" (permutation test p-value), "split" (values provided parameter split_labels), "feature" (values provided parameter feature_values). Default = NULL apply color scheme. permutation_test_alpha numeric value indicating significance level apply permutation test results. Splits pass threshold grayed . Default = 1 applies threshold. label_splits Boolean value indicating whether label splits. Defaults FALSE. label_statistics Boolean value indicating whether label values selected metric. Defaults FALSE. palette character string indicating palette name. Permitted values "choir", "archr", "inferno", "frozen". Default = NULL use "choir" discrete colors "inferno" gradient colors. Alternately, provide vector color values use starting values color palette. color_limits vector minimum maximum values indicated color_by ("n_sig" "pvalue") display color bar legend. Default = NULL sets limits automatically. fix_coords Boolean value indicating whether aspect ratio x y axis 1. ... Extra parameters passed Seurat::DimPlot() Seurat::FeaturePlot().

title: Plot dimensionality reduction — plotDimReduction

what: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
title: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
text: function identifies differentially expressed features two groups using indicated differential expression analysis methods.

title: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
code: runDE(   object,   metadata = NULL,   replicate_labels = NULL,   group_labels,   split_labels = NULL,   reference_group = NULL,   design = NULL,   use_cells = NULL,   pseudobulk = "generate",   de_method = "edgeR",   de_test = "LRT",   de_params = list(),   return_raw_de = FALSE,   normalize_prefilter = FALSE,   p_adjust_method = "fdr",   min_cells_per_split = 100,   min_cells_per_replicate = 10,   min_replicates_per_split = 6,   min_replicates_per_group = 3,   min_cells_per_feature = 10,   min_prop_cells_per_feature = 0.1,   force_balance = FALSE,   use_assay = NULL,   use_layer = NULL,   random_seed = 1,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
text: object object class Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, matrix. Data supplied class matrix may either feature x cell matrix pre-computed pseudobulk feature x replicate matrix. Note raw counts expected, normalization method applied differential expression analysis differs across methods tests. metadata optional dataframe containing relevant metadata columns corresponding data provided parameter object. Default = NULL looks metadata object provided inputs. replicate_labels string indicating name metadata column containing biological replicate labels vector containing biological replicate labels order. pseudobulk DE analysis, biological replicate labels used construct/define pseudobulks. Input required cell-level DE analysis. group_labels string indicating name metadata column containing two comparison group labels vector containing comparison labels order. split_labels string indicating name metadata column split cells prior pseudobulking performing differential expression (e.g., cell types). Alternately, vector containing split labels cell order. Results returned unique value indicated split_labels. Default = NULL run pseudobulk differential expression cells together. reference_group string specifying reference group. Defaults NULL, case first value alphabetically used reference. design optional string specifying model formula complex designs. Last term formula must correspond group labels. Default = NULL run pairwise group comparison (~ group) based input provided parameter group_labels. use_cells vector cell names subset object prior subsequent pseudobulk differential expression steps. Default = NULL use cells. pseudobulk string indicating pseudobulk handling. Permitted values : "generate" (pseudobulk matrices generated), "supplied" (pseudobulk matrix supplied user parameter object), "none" (pseudobulking used, cell-level differential expression analysis run). Defaults "generate". de_method tool use differential expression analysis. Permitted values "edgeR", "DESeq2", "limma", "presto", "BPCells". Defaults "edgeR". de_test test use differential expression analysis. Available values dependent de_method: "edgeR" ("LRT", "QLF", "exact"), "DESeq2" ("LRT", "Wald"), "limma" ("trend", "voom", "wilcox_cpm", "wilcox_log_cpm"), "presto" ("wilcox_cpm", "wilcox_log_cpm"), "BPCells" ("wilcox_cpm", "wilcox_log_cpm"). Defaults "LRT". de_params list lists containing additional parameters passed specific DE functions. name element must specific DE function parameters passed. Defaults empty list. special top-level option return_all_coefficients = TRUE can used return_raw_de = TRUE coefficient-based model tests include raw results model coefficients. return_raw_de Boolean value indicating whether also return raw output selected DE method/test. Defaults FALSE. normalize_prefilter Boolean value indicating whether normalization applied (TRUE) (FALSE) filtering features low counts. Defaults FALSE. p_adjust_method string indicating multiple comparison adjustment use. permitted values, see stats::p.adjust.methods. Defaults "fdr" (Benjamini & Hochberg, 1995). advanced users, parameter can also set "fdrtool" use fdrtool package (applied raw p-values default, DESeq2 Wald, apply z-scores setting parameter de_params list(fdrtools = list(statistic = "zscore"))). min_cells_per_split numeric value indicating minimum number cells within one split. Pseudobulk differential expression steps performed splits fewer cells. Defaults 100. min_cells_per_replicate numeric value indicating minimum number cells within one replicate one split. Pseudobulk steps performed replicates fewer cells split. Defaults 10. min_replicates_per_split numeric value indicating minimum number distinct replicates represented within one split. Pseudobulk expression differential expression performed splits fewer replicates. Defaults 6. min_replicates_per_group numeric value indicating minimum number distinct replicates represented within two comparison groups. Pseudobulk differential expression steps performed splits fewer replicates. Defaults 3. min_cells_per_feature numeric value indicating minimum number cells (within split) expression feature. Pseudobulk differential expression calculated features expressed fewer cells. Defaults 10. min_prop_cells_per_feature numeric value indicating minimum proportion cells (within split) expression feature. Pseudobulk differential expression calculated features expressed fewer cells. Defaults 0.1. force_balance boolean indicating whether force two comparison groups sample size. Defaults FALSE. TRUE, larger group randomly downsampled size smaller group. use_assay string indicating assay use provided object. Default = NULL choose current active assay Seurat objects counts assay SingleCellExperiment objects. use_layer Seurat objects, string vector indicating layer (previously known slot) use provided object. Default = NULL use counts layer. random_seed numerical value indicating random seed used. Defaults 1. relevant function parameter force_balance = TRUE. n_cores numeric value indicating number cores use parallelization. Default = NULL use number available cores minus 2. verbose Boolean value indicating whether use verbose output execution function. Defaults TRUE. Can set FALSE cleaner output.

title: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
text: Returns list containing following elements: DE_results Dataframe containing DE results feature, split PB_values using pseudobulk data, list feature x replicate matri(ces) containing pseudobulk values feature, one matrix per split cell_values Alternately, using cell-level data, list feature x cell matri(ces) containing counts feature, one matrix per split metadata List recording characteristics data runtime parameters List recording parameter values used

title: Run differential expression analysis to compare two groups — runDE
text: default, pseudobulk matri(ces) generated supplied user, used run pseudobulk differential expression. following existing tools supported: edgeR, DESeq2, limma, Wilcoxon rank-sum test. Alternately, users may skip pseudobulking run cell-level differential expression (recommended cases).
